title: Polystichum caruifolium
taxonomy: Polystichum caruifolium belongs to the class Polypodiopsida, which is a group of ferns. This classification is based on its unique characteristics and genetic makeup. As a member of the family Dryopteridaceae, Polystichum caruifolium is closely related to other species within this family. Polystichum caruifolium's classification is further supported by its morphological features, such as its leaf shape and growth habit.
uses: Polystichum caruifolium, also known as the Japanese holly fern, has been traditionally used in some parts of the world for its medicinal properties. Its leaves and stems are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may be beneficial in treating various health conditions.
